The state railway commission
has written to the chamber of
commerce that it will take up any
| excessive or unfair charges in
freights imposed by the railroads
entering this city.


President Fassett of the chamber
has asked that all such charges
be filed with the Spokane Mer-
chants association, which will care
for them and have them presented
to the state railway commission by
it's rate expert, j. H. Kane. Mr..
Kane will leave for Washington to-
! day to be present at the hearings


of the Spokane freight rate case.
Because of new street paving, the
| wooden main on Perry street, south
of 25th avenue is being removed
today by the water department and
the following district is without
water: On Perry street from
Ninth to Seventeeth avenue; in the
district easty of Perry street be-
tween Tenth and Seventeenth ave-
nues, and in the district between
Perry and Conklin streets, Tenth
to Eighteenth avenues.


r. e. Strahorn and his North
| coast directors have incorporated
a new railway line to run from
Couer d'Alene, Ida, to Lewiston.
| The incorporation has been filed
for the sum of 100000 and gives
the road the right to use steam
or electricity.

Refusal on the part Of Dr.. F. J.
| Whitaker to permit the sweating
of George Easter at st. Luke's hos
pital, has caused the continuance
of the case against William Ward
in the police court. The police
wanted to get at Casters side of
the case, in which it is alleged
that he was murderously assaulted
by ward but the doctor would not
permit it. 1t is thought that Eas.
ter can tell his story before the
